channel.title: math.CV updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.CV updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Intrinsic \(q\)-Radial Vector Derivatives and Localized Fischer Decompositions on Radial Algebras
item.description: arXiv:2605.00775v2 Announce Type: new Abstract: We construct an intrinsic q-deformation of the vector derivative on radial algebras. The construction is not obtained from a coordinate realization by replacing ordinary partial derivatives with one-variable Jackson derivatives; that coordinatewise procedure does not preserve radial subalgebras. Instead, for each distinguished vector variable $x$ and each finite set of auxiliary variables $Y\subset S\setminus\{x\}$, we define a q-Cartan derivative $\partial^Y_{x,q}$ on $R(\{x\}\cup Y)$ using the $x$-relative scalar variables $x^2$ and $\{x,y\}$, $y\in Y$. We prove two Fischer-type theorems. First, an exterior Fischer operator has a triangular anticommutator with explicit resonance factors; after inverting them one obtains a global Green operator and an exterior direct-sum decomposition. Second, using full left multiplication by $x$, we prove the monogenic Fischer decomposition after localization by finite-block determinants. We also describe the first denominator factors: the one-vector and two-vector factors are explicit, while the general determinant factors split by $x$-support. A degree-zero support-rank obstruction shows that a universal unlocalized theorem for all real $0<q<1$ cannot hold without excluding q-resonances.
item.dc:creator: Diana Barseghyan (Schneiderov\'a), Juan Bory-Reyes, Baruch Schneider, Yifan Zhang

item.title: Valuative independence and metric SYZ conjecture
item.description: arXiv:2605.00516v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Given a polarised maximal degeneration of compact Calabi-Yau manifolds, assuming there exists a canonical basis of the section ring for the polarisation line bundle, satisfying the valuative independence condition, we will prove the metric SYZ conjecture.
item.dc:creator: Yang Li

item.title: Schur-Agler class and Carath\'eodory extremal functions
item.description: arXiv:2510.17658v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We study the role of Carath\'eodory extremal functions in the Schur-Agler class generated by a collection of test functions. We show that under certain conditions, $\mathbb{D}$ and $\mathbb{D}^2$ are the only domains where finitely many test functions can generate the Schur class. As applications, we give a description of the Carath\'eodory extremals in the unit ball of the multiplier algebra of the Drury-Arveson space and give operator-theoretic Herglotz representations for any Carath\'eodory hyperbolic domain.
item.dc:creator: Anindya Biswas

item.title: A Comparison Test for Meromorphic Extensions
item.description: arXiv:2601.05896v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We provide a comparison test for meromorphic extensions, i.e., if two series are ``close enough" then the existence of a meromorphic extension of one to the entire complex plane ensures a similar extension for the other. We use this result to generate new examples of Dirichlet series admitting meromorphic extensions. Moreover, we demonstrate that our requirements are optimal by constructing a collection of counterexamples where the series are ``close but not enough": one series admits a meromorphic extension while the other possesses a natural boundary.
item.dc:creator: Adi Gl\"ucksam, Yuzhou Joey Zou

item.title: Energy estimates for level sets of holomorphic functions and universal counterexamples to Calder\'on-Zygmund theory
item.description: arXiv:2604.01559v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We demonstrate that the failure of $L^1$ regularity in Calder\'on-Zygmund theory is a universal phenomenon: every non-constant holomorphic function in $\C^n$ generates a counterexample to the Poisson equation. In order to achieve this goal, we shall establish sharp level-set estimates that link harmonic analysis to the geometry of complex structure through Hironaka's resolution of singularities and the \L{}ojasiewicz gradient inequality.
item.dc:creator: Yifei Pan, Guokuan Shao, Jianfei Wang, Jujie Wu

item.title: $A^p_\alpha$ classes in the Dirichlet range: inner-outer factorization, Carleson measures and weak products
item.description: arXiv:2604.21347v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We study properties of $A^p_\alpha$ spaces in the Dirichlet range, recently defined by Brevig, Kulikov, Seip and Zlotnikov as the set of all holomorphic functions on the unit disc $\mathbb{D}$ such that \[ \int_{\mathbb{D}} |f(z)|^{p-2} |f'(z)|^2 (1 - |z|^2)^{\alpha} \, dA(z) < \infty, \] when $0<\alpha < 1$ and $p > 0$. We answer in the negative two questions posed by Brevig et al. by showing that, if $p\ne2$ and $p > \frac{1}{2}$, $A^p_\alpha$ is not a vector space and that the norm is in general not increasing in $p$. This is achieved by means of an equivalent description for $A^p_\alpha$ which is given in terms of the Poisson integral of the boundary function of its inhabitants. Such norm also leads to a description of $A^p_\alpha$ functions in the Dirichlet range given in terms of their inner and outer factors. As a corollary, we show that $A^1_\alpha$ is contained in the weak product of a Dirichlet-type space.
item.dc:creator: Alberto Dayan, Adri\'an Llinares, Miguel Monsalve-L\'opez
